title: Stock Market Today Live, July 17: Sensex jumps 965 pts, Nifty ends above 24,334; financials rally ahead of key earnings - The HinduBusinessLine
text:
Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights - Markets were led by IT and financial stocks after upbeat ‌results from Tech Mahindra and Jio Financial, ‌swinging to weekly ‌gains ⁠despite trading in their ⁠narrowest range of 2026.

Rupee dropped 0.07% versus the U.S. dollar to ‌96.28, logging its sharpest weekly drop since May, as elevated oil prices ‌and strong merchant dollar demand renewed worries about the currency’s ⁠trajectory.

The benchmark 10-year bond was quoted at 101.22 ‌rupees, with the yield up 2 basis points at 6.7659%.

Banking and financial stocks shine ahead of key earnings: HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Axis Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, PNB, IDBI Bank, Yes Bank to announce results tomorrow.

Fast-delivery company Zepto Ltd. is seeking a valuation in an initial public offering sharply below its $7 billion peak as concerns over cash burn temper optimism around the industry and raise questions about the firm’s path to profitability.

Zepto aims for a lower IPO valuation of $4.5 billion amid concerns over profitability and cash burn in the industry.

State-owned NLC India’s subsidiary NLC India Renewables Ltd (NIRL) has appointed four leading investment banks, including SBI Capital Markets and HDFC Bank, as book running lead managers for its proposed initial public offering (IPO).

State-owned NLC India's subsidiary NLC India Renewables Ltd (NIRL) has appointed four leading investment banks, including SBI Capital Markets and HDFC Bank, as book running lead managers for its proposed initial public offering (IPO).

The Indian rupee logged its sharpest weekly drop since May, with Friday’s uneventful trading session closing out the week, as ‌elevated oil prices and strong merchant dollar demand renewed worries about the currency’s trajectory.

The Indian rupee experiences its steepest weekly drop in nine weeks, closing at 96.28 per dollar amid rising oil prices.

JSW Steel on Friday said its consolidated net profit more than doubled to Rs 4,696 crore in the quarter ended June 30, supported by increase in revenues.

JSW Steel on Friday said its consolidated net profit more than doubled to Rs 4,696 crore in the quarter ended June 30, supported by increase in revenues.

Sensex settled 964.58 pts or 1.25% higher at 78,151.45; and Nifty 50 climbed 261.55 pts or 1.09% to 24,334.30.

Banking and financial stocks shine ahead of key earnings: HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Axis Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, PNB, IDBI Bank, Yes Bank to announce results tomorrow.

Consumer electrical goods maker Havells India Ltd on Friday reported a 16.64 per cent decline in consolidated net profit at Rs 289.71 crore in the first quarter ended June 30, 2026, hit by higher raw material and input costs due to West Asia war and higher spending on advertising and promotion.

Revenue from operations in the first quarter stood at ₹6,518.19 crore as against ₹5,455.35 crore in the year-ago period

Kalyani Steels: Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has conditionally revoked the closure directions issued earlier. Accordingly, the Company has resumed manufacturing operations at its Plant located at Hospet Road, Village Ginigera, Talula & District Koppal, Karnataka

Allcargo Terminals’ total volumes in June 2026 were 55.6 ‘000 TEUs – 4% higher vs June’25 & 10% lower over May’26.

Navkar Corporation reported net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 at Rs 12.28 crore, compared to Rs 2.45 crore in the same quarter last year.

Jindal Stainless said it has supplied 40 per cent of the total stainless steel requirement for India’s first hydrogen-powered train, flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday.

Jindal Stainless said it has supplied 40 per cent of the total stainless steel requirement for India's first hydrogen-powered train, flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday.

United Breweries Limited Commissions New Canning Line at Nizam Brewery, Strengthening Premium Portfolio and Supply Chain Capabilities

JSW Steel reported standalone net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 at Rs 2,826 crore as against Rs 2,217 crore in the same quarter last year.

Central Bank of India reports a 13% profit increase to ₹1,324 crore in Q1, alongside improved asset quality.

Waaree Energies has received an order for supply of 212 MW solar modules from a renowned customer that owns and manages utility-scale renewable power projects

Globus Spirits reported standalone net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 at Rs 27.55 crore compared to Rs 18.52 crore in the same quarter last year.

Shares traded 3% lower at Rs 958.55 on the NSE.

Orkla India: MTR, one of India’s most trusted food brands with a legacy spanning over 100 years, announced the launch of its Protein Breakfast Range, further strengthening its leadership in the breakfast category through consumer-led innovation

Shakti Pumps (India) has invested Rs. 5.00 Crores in its wholly owned subsidiary i.e. Shakti EV Mobility Private Limited.

“This could be the most consequential shift in India’s digital payments landscape since the zero-MDR mandate in 2020. While the proposed 5–7 bps MDR would apply to only 2–4% of merchants, those above ₹1–1.5 crore in annual turnover, this segment drives an outsized share of UPI’s merchant transaction value. For banks, PSPs and payment infrastructure providers who have absorbed processing costs for years without direct monetisation, this could unlock a sustainable revenue model, potentially generating ₹3,500–5,000 crore annually, well above what the government previously spent subsidising the ecosystem. Crucially, small merchants and P2P transactions stay untouched, so the inclusion gains of the last five years aren’t at risk.”

Havells India’s standalone net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 declined 15.3% to Rs 298.43 crore as against Rs 352.34 crore in the same quarter last year.

Shares traded at Rs 1,154.70 on the NSE, down 1%

Bondada Engineering has received a purchase order from Veremax Technologie Services Limited for t
